description: Add multi-PRD parallel execution via git worktrees and OpenRouter configuration panel in VS Code extension

qualityChecks.name: syntax
qualityChecks.command: node --check src/core/openrouter.js src/core/worktree.js src/commands/worktree.js src/mcp/server.js src/cli.js src/core/config.js src/core/runner.js 2>&1 || true

userStories.title: OpenRouter config module in Config class
userStories.description: Extend src/core/config.js with methods to manage .ralph/config.json — a gitignored file that stores OpenRouter API key and enabled models. This is the foundation for all OpenRouter features.
acceptanceCriteria: Add configFile property to Config constructor pointing to join(prdDir, 'config.json')
Add loadGlobalConfig() that reads and parses config.json, returns {} if missing
Add saveGlobalConfig(data) that writes config.json with 2-space indentation
Add getOpenRouterKey() that returns key from config.json, falls back to process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY
Add getEnabledModels() that returns array of enabled model objects from config.json
Add .ralph/.gitignore entry for config.json and .openrouter-models-cache.json
config.json schema: { openrouter: { apiKey, defaultModel, researchModel, models: [{ id, enabled }] } }
userStories.notes: Only modify src/core/config.js and .ralph/.gitignore. Do NOT add npm dependencies.

userStories.title: OpenRouter API client module
userStories.description: Create src/core/openrouter.js with functions to validate API keys, fetch available models from OpenRouter API, and cache results locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/core/openrouter.js as ESM module
validateApiKey(apiKey) — test key with GET /models, return boolean
fetchAvailableModels(apiKey) — GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models, return array of {id, name, contextLength, pricing}
fetchModelsWithCache(apiKey, cacheFile) — read cache from cacheFile if < 1h old, otherwise fetch and write cache
All functions use Node.js built-in fetch (no npm deps)
Sort models by id alphabetically
userStories.notes: Zero dependencies. Use Node.js 18+ built-in fetch. Cache file is .ralph/.openrouter-models-cache.json.

userStories.title: Update runner to read API key from config.json
userStories.description: Modify src/core/runner.js so spawnOpenRouter() and runResearch() read the API key from config.json first (via Config), falling back to OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnOpenRouter() calls getOpenRouterKey() from a Config instance to get API key
runResearch() calls getOpenRouterKey() from a Config instance to get API key
If config.json has apiKey, use it even if env var is not set
If neither config.json nor env var has key, fall back to Claude Sonnet with warning
Import Config and findPrdDir from the correct modules
Do NOT break existing env var behavior — it still works as fallback
userStories.notes: Modify src/core/runner.js only. The runner needs to instantiate Config to read the key. Import findPrdDir from ../utils.js.

userStories.title: MCP tools for OpenRouter configuration
userStories.description: Add MCP tools to src/mcp/server.js for managing OpenRouter settings: get/set config, list enabled models, fetch available models from API, toggle model enabled state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ralph_openrouter_config tool — get or set OpenRouter config (apiKey masked in response, defaultModel, researchModel)
Add ralph_openrouter_models tool — list enabled models from config.json
Add ralph_openrouter_fetch_models tool — fetch models from OpenRouter API (requires apiKey), returns list with id/name/pricing
Add ralph_openrouter_toggle_model tool — enable/disable a model by id in config.json
All tools follow existing patterns: compact JSON responses, error handling with try/catch
Add tools to the TOOLS array with proper inputSchema definitions
userStories.notes: Modify src/mcp/server.js only. Follow the exact same patterns as existing tools. Mask API key in responses (show only last 4 chars).

userStories.title: Git worktree core module
userStories.description: Create src/core/worktree.js with a WorktreeManager class that handles creating, removing, listing git worktrees. Each worktree gets its own .ralph/ directory with isolated PRD state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/core/worktree.js with WorktreeManager class
Constructor takes prdDir, derives projectRoot and worktreesDir (.ralph-worktrees/)
loadRegistry()/saveRegistry() — CRUD for .ralph/worktrees.json
create(name, branch) — git worktree add, init .ralph/ in worktree, copy codebase patterns, register
remove(name) — check not running, git worktree remove, deregister
list() — return enriched worktree entries with live status (idle/running/complete) from lock/status files
getConfig(name) — return Config instance pointing to worktree .ralph/
startRun(name, maxIterations, tool) — spawn ralph run detached in worktree dir
startAll(maxIterations, tool) — start all pending worktrees in parallel
merge(name) — verify complete, git merge branch, remove worktree
Ensure .ralph-worktrees/ is added to project .gitignore
userStories.notes: Zero dependencies. Use child_process.execSync for git commands, spawn for ralph run. The worktree dir is .ralph-worktrees/ at project root (not inside .ralph/).

userStories.title: CLI command: ralph worktree
userStories.description: Create src/commands/worktree.js with subcommands and integrate into src/cli.js router.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/worktree.js with worktreeCommand(args) function
Subcommands: create <name> --branch <branch>, remove <name>, list, status, run <name> [--max-iterations N], run-all [--max-iterations N], merge <name>
Integrate in src/cli.js: add 'worktree' case to switch, add parseWorktreeArgs function
Add worktree to HELP text in cli.js
list/status output uses ANSI colors and progress bars from utils.js
run-all shows a consolidated status of all parallel loops
userStories.notes: Modify src/cli.js and create src/commands/worktree.js. Use WorktreeManager from src/core/worktree.js. Follow existing CLI patterns.

userStories.title: MCP tools for worktree management
userStories.description: Add MCP tools to src/mcp/server.js for creating, listing, running, and managing worktrees via Claude Code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ralph_worktree_create tool — create worktree with name and branch
Add ralph_worktree_remove tool — remove worktree by name
Add ralph_worktree_list tool — list all worktrees with status and progress
Add ralph_worktree_run tool — start ralph run in a specific worktree
Add ralph_worktree_run_all tool — start all pending worktrees in parallel
Add ralph_worktree_create_prd tool — create prd.json inside a worktree
Add ralph_worktree_merge tool — merge completed worktree branch
All tools follow existing MCP patterns with compact JSON responses
userStories.notes: Modify src/mcp/server.js only. Import WorktreeManager from ../core/worktree.js. Follow the exact same tool definition patterns.

userStories.title: VS Code: OpenRouter settings panel
userStories.description: Create a webview panel in the VS Code extension for configuring OpenRouter API key and selecting available models.
acceptanceCriteria: Create vscode/src/views/openrouter-panel.ts as a WebviewPanel (singleton pattern like DashboardPanel)
Section 1: API key input (password type with show/hide toggle), Validate button, Save button
Section 2: Fetch Models button that queries OpenRouter API, model list with checkboxes to enable/disable
Dropdown for default model and research model selection
Register command ralph.openRouterSettings in extension.ts and vscode/package.json
Add button in Stories view title bar to open the panel
Panel reads/writes .ralph/config.json through RalphConfig
Use VS Code theme variables for styling (--vscode-* CSS vars)
userStories.notes: Follow the DashboardPanel singleton pattern. The panel communicates with extension via postMessage. Add loadGlobalConfig/saveGlobalConfig methods to vscode/src/ralph/config.ts.

userStories.title: VS Code: Model selector in story creation + model badges in tree
userStories.description: Update the story creation flow to show OpenRouter models from config.json and add model badges to the stories tree view.
acceptanceCriteria: In vscode/src/commands/stories.ts addStory(): show built-in models (haiku/sonnet/opus) plus enabled OpenRouter models from config.json in QuickPick
QuickPick items show model name as label and pricing info as description
OpenRouter models shown with 'openrouter:' prefix automatically added to story.model
In vscode/src/views/stories-tree.ts: show model name in StoryItem description
Add OpenRouterConfig interface to vscode/src/ralph/types.ts
Add loadGlobalConfig() to vscode/src/ralph/config.ts to read .ralph/config.json
userStories.notes: Modify vscode/src/commands/stories.ts, vscode/src/views/stories-tree.ts, vscode/src/ralph/config.ts, and vscode/src/ralph/types.ts.

userStories.title: VS Code: Worktrees tree view and controls
userStories.description: Add a Worktrees section to the Ralph sidebar showing all active worktrees with status, progress, and controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create vscode/src/views/worktrees-tree.ts with WorktreesTreeProvider (TreeDataProvider)
Show each worktree as tree item with: name, branch, status icon (idle/running/complete), progress percentage
Context menu: Start Loop, Stop, Remove, Open Folder, Merge (if complete)
Header button: Create Worktree (opens multi-step input: name, branch)
Register view ralph.worktrees in vscode/package.json under ralph viewsContainer
Register commands: ralph.createWorktree, ralph.removeWorktree, ralph.startWorktreeLoop, ralph.mergeWorktree
Auto-refresh when worktrees.json or worktree status files change
Add worktree commands to extension.ts
userStories.notes: Follow StoriesTreeProvider patterns. The tree reads .ralph/worktrees.json and each worktree's .ralph/status.txt for live status. Add RalphConfig methods for worktree management.

userStories.title: Activity logger module + per-story log files
userStories.description: Create src/core/activity.js with an ActivityLogger class that writes structured JSONL events to .ralph/activity.jsonl and saves per-story agent output to .ralph/logs/<story-id>.log.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/core/activity.js as ESM module with ActivityLogger class
Constructor takes prdDir, creates .ralph/logs/ directory if needed
emit(event) method — appends JSON line to .ralph/activity.jsonl with timestamp
Event types: story_start, agent_spawn, agent_done, quality_check, story_done, feedback_received, loop_start, loop_end, error
startStoryLog(storyId) — creates/truncates .ralph/logs/<storyId>.log, returns writable stream
appendStoryLog(storyId, chunk) — appends text to the story log file
readStoryLog(storyId) — reads full log for a story
readActivity(limit) — reads last N events from activity.jsonl (tail)
clear() — truncates activity.jsonl (for new runs)
Add activity.jsonl and logs/ to .ralph/.gitignore
userStories.notes: Zero dependencies. Use Node.js fs built-ins. JSONL = one JSON object per line, no array wrapping. Each line has { ts, type, ...data }.

userStories.title: Integrate activity logger in runner + run.js + feedback mechanism
userStories.description: Wire the ActivityLogger into the main loop (run.js) and runner.js to emit events at each stage. Add feedback mechanism: read .ralph/.feedback before each iteration, include in prompt, then clear.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/commands/run.js: create ActivityLogger instance at start
Emit loop_start event when loop begins
Emit story_start event with story id, title, model, effort, iteration number
Emit agent_spawn event when spawnAgent is called
Emit agent_done event when agent finishes with exit code and duration
Emit story_done event when story is marked as passed
Emit loop_end event when loop finishes
Save agent output to .ralph/logs/<storyId>.log after each agent run
In runner.js: tee agent stdout to a callback/stream so run.js can capture it for the log file
Before each iteration, read .ralph/.feedback — if non-empty, prepend to prompt as '## User Feedback' section
Clear .ralph/.feedback after reading it
Add .feedback to .ralph/.gitignore
userStories.notes: Modify src/commands/run.js and src/core/runner.js. Import ActivityLogger from ../core/activity.js. The runner's spawnProcess needs to accept an optional onData callback so run.js can stream output to the log file. Do NOT break existing terminal tee behavior.

userStories.title: VS Code: Live Activity Panel with history and feedback
userStories.description: Replace/enhance the Progress panel in the VS Code extension with a rich Live Activity Panel that shows real-time events, story history with log access, and a feedback input for user intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or replace vscode/src/views/progress-panel.ts with enhanced webview
Top section: current story card (id, title, model, effort, iteration, elapsed time)
Middle section: scrollable activity log reading from .ralph/activity.jsonl with auto-scroll and color-coded event types
Activity events rendered with icons: story_start (●), agent_spawn (↳), agent_done (✓/✗), quality_check (✓/✗), story_done (✓), feedback_received (💬)
Bottom section: feedback text input with Send button — writes to .ralph/.feedback
Control buttons: Pause (SIGTSTP), Skip Story (kill agent), Stop Loop (SIGINT)
History section: list of completed stories with duration, files changed info, and 'View Log' button that opens .ralph/logs/<storyId>.log in editor
Auto-refresh by watching activity.jsonl for changes (FileSystemWatcher)
Use VS Code theme CSS variables for styling
Register any new commands needed in extension.ts and package.json
userStories.notes: Follow existing webview patterns from progress-panel.ts and dashboard-panel.ts. The panel communicates with extension via postMessage. For feedback, the extension writes to .ralph/.feedback. For controls (pause/skip/stop), reuse terminal signal logic from commands/loop.ts. Activity log should auto-scroll to bottom but allow user to scroll up without jumping.

userStories.title: Core timing: per-story duration tracking + ETA calculation
userStories.description: Track timing data per story in prd.json and add an ETA calculation function that estimates remaining time based on completed story durations grouped by effort/model category.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/commands/run.js: write startedAt (ISO string) to story in prd.json when story begins
In src/commands/run.js: write completedAt (ISO string) and durationMs (number) to story in prd.json when story finishes
Track loopStartedAt timestamp at the beginning of the run loop

Create calculateEta(data) function in src/utils.js that: groups completed story durations by effort+model category, calculates average per category, estimates remaining stories using category average or fallback defaults
Fallback defaults when no history: low/sonnet=2m, low/opus=4m, medium/sonnet=5m, medium/opus=10m, high/sonnet=10m, high/opus=20m
Update formatStatus() to include elapsed time and ETA in status.txt: append '| elapsed Xm | eta ~Ym' to the status line
calculateEta returns { elapsedMs, etaMs, etaFormatted, avgPerCategory }
userStories.notes: Modify src/commands/run.js and src/utils.js. Use config.updateStory() to write timing fields. The status.txt format change must be backward-compatible — existing parsers that split on ' | ' should still work (just more fields). Do NOT break the existing status parsing in the VS Code extension.

userStories.title: Display timing: elapsed + ETA in CLI status and VS Code panels
userStories.description: Update all display surfaces to show elapsed time and estimated time remaining: CLI ralph status command, VS Code status bar, and VS Code progress panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/commands/status.js to show elapsed and ETA when loop is running (parse from status.txt)
Update vscode/src/views/status-bar.ts to show elapsed and ETA in the status bar tooltip and text
Update vscode/src/views/progress-panel.ts to show elapsed time and ETA card in the webview
Update vscode/src/ralph/types.ts StatusInfo interface to include elapsed and eta optional string fields
Update parseStatus() in vscode/src/ralph/config.ts to extract elapsed and eta from the extended status.txt format
ETA should show as '~Xm' or '~Xh Ym' format, elapsed as 'Xm' or 'Xh Ym'
When loop is not running or no timing data, gracefully hide timing info instead of showing zeros
userStories.notes: Modify src/commands/status.js, vscode/src/views/status-bar.ts, vscode/src/views/progress-panel.ts, vscode/src/ralph/config.ts, vscode/src/ralph/types.ts. The status.txt extended format is: 'done/total (pct%) | story_id | status | iter x/y | time | elapsed Xm | eta ~Ym'. Parse the last two pipe-separated fields if present."

userStories.title: Global registry for active Ralph loops
userStories.description: Create a global registry at ~/.ralph/registry.json where each ralph run registers itself on start and deregisters on finish. This enables cross-project monitoring from the Hub.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/core/registry.js with GlobalRegistry class
Constructor uses ~/.ralph/registry.json (create ~/.ralph/ dir if needed)
register(entry) — adds { project, branch, projectPath, prdDir, pid, startedAt } to registry
deregister(pid) — removes entry by PID
list() — returns all entries, filtering out stale PIDs (process.kill(pid, 0) check)
prune() — removes all stale entries and saves
In src/commands/run.js: call register() after acquireLock(), call deregister() in finally block
Registry file is append-safe (read-modify-write with JSON)
Handle concurrent access gracefully (read fresh before write)
userStories.notes: Zero dependencies. Use os.homedir() for ~/.ralph/. The registry must survive crashes — always prune stale PIDs on read. Do NOT break existing run.js flow."

userStories.title: Hub server: unified web dashboard for all Ralph loops
userStories.description: Create src/hub/server.js — an HTTP server that serves a unified dashboard UI, REST API, and SSE stream for monitoring and controlling all active Ralph loops across projects. Accessible from browser and VS Code webview alike.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hub/server.js with startHub(opts) function
HTTP server on port 3742 (configurable via --port)
GET / — serves dashboard HTML with real-time UI showing all active loops
GET /events — SSE stream pushing status updates for all loops (poll registry + status.txt every 2s)
GET /api/status — JSON array of all active loops with progress, current story, elapsed, eta
GET /api/logs/:project/:storyId — returns story log content from .ralph/logs/<storyId>.log
POST /api/feedback — write feedback to target project's .ralph/.feedback file
POST /api/control — start/stop/skip/pause a loop (send signals to PID from registry)
Dashboard HTML: dark theme, cards per project with progress bar, current story, elapsed/eta, activity log, feedback input, control buttons
SSE events auto-update the dashboard without page refresh
Basic auth via RALPH_HUB_TOKEN env var (optional — if set, require Authorization header)
Add 'hub' command to src/cli.js with --port and --token options
Zero npm dependencies — use Node.js built-in http module
userStories.notes: Follow the existing dashboard/server.js patterns (inline HTML, SSE, Node.js http). The hub reads ~/.ralph/registry.json for active loops, then reads each project's .ralph/status.txt and .ralph/prd.json for detailed info. Dashboard must work standalone in browser AND be embeddable in VS Code webview. Keep HTML self-contained (no external assets)."

userStories.title: VS Code: Hub panel connecting to hub server
userStories.description: Add a WebviewPanel to the VS Code extension that connects to the Ralph Hub server, showing the same unified dashboard inside VS Code for monitoring and controlling all active loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Create vscode/src/views/hub-panel.ts with HubPanel class (singleton pattern like DashboardPanel)
On open: check if hub server is running (fetch http://localhost:3742/api/status), show start prompt if not
Load dashboard HTML from hub server and render in webview, or show fallback UI if hub not reachable
Forward postMessage events from webview to hub API (feedback, control actions)
Register command ralph.showHub in extension.ts and vscode/package.json
Add Hub button to Ralph activity bar or Stories view title
Auto-reconnect if hub server restarts
Show notification when a loop completes or fails (poll /api/status or listen to SSE)
userStories.notes: Follow DashboardPanel singleton pattern. The webview loads the same HTML served by the hub server. Use vscode.env.openExternal as fallback to open in browser if webview has issues. Register command in vscode/package.json contributes.commands."
